storyInfo: The token that Zuo Le retrieves from the Garden of Grotesqueries turns out to be a sliver of Jie's lifeforce, before which Chun's pride and principles fall to pieces. Jun entrusts the writing knife to Ch'en. Zuo and Mo head for the Tomb of Sui, but they are stopped by Ya.

attributes.content: You almost hit a bookshelf.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I am no swordswoman and I have no interest in admiring Chi Xiao. But it would be a shame if your sword ruins the collection of the Sky Mirror Pavilion.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: But you are not confident about taking the token from me.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Is that so?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: The Elf now truly waves her jade scepter and twig for the first time.
Withered vines rush out from the ground, forming massive hands.
Dammit, she's a nightmare to deal with! Is the Sui Regulator's Supervisor decided through combat?!
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: How does the book go anyway... ah, I remember—and it's autumn, this is perfect...
attributes.name: Mo Yi
Mo Yi
attributes.content: 'Metal's nature is change.'

attributes.name: Mo Yi
attributes.content: Take this!
For a thief, you're pretty well-learned.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I wouldn't be surprised if I know Yin-Yang and the Five Elements better than someone who's forgotten her roots.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Zuo Le! Ch'en Hui-chieh! Your turn!
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Zuo Le charges towards Chun, intent on disrupting her ability to respond.
Meanwhile, Ch'en Hui-chieh pulls back, waiting for an opportunity to strike.
In between them, Mo Yi unleashes razor-sharp autumn winds, tangling with the vine hands.
The Supervisor of the Sui Regulator continues to stand where she is, utterly impassive, as though she is watching a farce that she is becoming increasingly bored with.
Ch'en Hui-chieh continues to back off, until her back is almost touching the closed door.
Damn... can't hold on much longer!
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Ch'en!
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Chi Xiao...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Riding on the autumn wind, the sword aura sweeps away all the vines. The Elf looks surprised for a brief moment.
Then, dried leaves sharp as razors fly out from the vines towards the trio.
Mo Yi urgently casts a defensive spell, but the leaves cut their clothes and bags open...
The token that has slumbered since it was retrieved from the Garden of Grotesqueries falls on the ground.
This is the token from the Garden of Grotesqueries?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: It is a geist.
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: It is, indeed, a geist.
The weiqi player had taken Sui's wildest imagination from the Garden of Grotesqueries and hollowed out the interior, leaving only the gleaming exterior...
The memories of his siblings were then injected to create this object.
Made of a glass-like substance, the tiny geist lies immobile on the ground for a while... then, it seems to grow ethereal.
It awakens.
It does not seem to feel pain. Or perhaps, the pain was overcome by the urge to return to this familiar place.
She begins to stumble towards the door.
That is...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Impossible!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: All it takes is a single thought from the Elf, and the churning vines could shatter the object to smithereens. But Chun cannot bring herself to do it.
Because she truly does see a familiar figure in it.
The object touches the door, and it is as though a dream that has slumbered for a hundred years is suddenly awakened by an intruder.
The falling dust forms thousands of golden threads drifting in the air in the setting sun. The sliver of life force ripples.
All breaths are held as they see the ethereal figure that drifts between light and shadow in the falling dust.
It is blurry, indistinct, the only clear feature its grey robe, like paper that has grown yellow from the passage of time, but still smelling faintly of old books and dried ink...
The door opens slowly.
How do you get in, if you give me the key?
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Why should the master of the house need a key?
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: The shadow bends slightly, as though trying to pick up the cracked scroll on the desk.
Or perhaps it is walking towards the window to open the sash closed for so long, to see if the osmanthus that was young back then has bloomed this year.
But her movement ultimately freezes between the done and the undone, a faint wisp of melancholy suspended in the sunset.
Put this scroll on the shelf by the wall. Leave that document on the desk; it's not yet finished.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: A painting from Dusk? I should find a good place to hang it.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Why do I have to do these chores for you?!
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Oh? But Nian said her Candleholder even helps her put together mahjong games.
attributes.name: Jie

attributes.content: Desk, chair and scrolls... every object in the room breathes with the shadow. They are her old friends, and she is the reason they never left.
A gust of wind blows through the room. The geist falls asleep and the shadow dissipates like a drop of ink that falls into water, leaving behind only that which is real, the shadows of dust.
And the sun, shining into an empty room.
It's...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Her...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: No, she was a little taller, her hair a little blacker... she was thin, but not like that...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: She cannot, would not accept, yet there is a single question that she could never answer.
Who else could it be?
Who else could it be?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: The stunned Elf does not even notice the appearance of the proxy, one who is very much flesh and blood.
She continues to stand at the same spot, staring in the direction where the shadow disappeared.
I came as you asked.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: But it seems you no longer have anything to say to me.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: It's her... it's really her...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: I never thought... I didn't think there was any trace of her existence left...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: There is no doubt about it. It's a sliver of her life force, which those young people retrieved from the Sui's Remnant Consciousness.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: As for traces of her existence... they are in the books of the Sky Mirror Pavilion, in countless scholars across the land of Yan.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Now, do you still believe that everything we siblings have done since Jie's disappearance is futile, Candleholder Chun?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: If... if you can still find a trace of her somewhere...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: Tell her...
attributes.name: Chun

attributes.content: The long-lived Candleholder chokes on her own words. She gazes wordlessly at the study, the interior of which has not changed for a century.
Having taken it all in, she turns around and slowly walks towards the exit, fighting the urge to look back at every step.
Leaving this place she knows best, yet brings her the most despair.
...I did not know that Wang's obsession was this paranoid, yet so deep.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I saw you at the city gates...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: And we meet again.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: You are... Jun?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Thank you for protecting her token.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Lady Jun... no, Master Jun.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: I heard you were a close friend of the grandmaster's, and I have long heard your name spoken of with reference... it is an honor to meet you.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Don't try to curry favor with me. I'm not even sure if I had been born at the time of the old carpenter's death.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I'm glad to see that someone still remembers his views after a thousand years have passed.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: But did you do what you did at Mangshan Town out of a desire for peace, or out of your own bitter cynicism? Do you really need me to tell you?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: The old Tianshi gave you an opportunity to make up for your transgressions. Have you done so?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: The proxy does not raise her voice, and there is no anger in it, but her voice naturally begets respect.
I've long heard of the second Sui sister's unapproachable nature. Today, I got to witness it.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: There is always hope for those who learn from their mistakes.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Zuo Le.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Academician Jun...
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Words cannot express my gratitude for you bringing back this sliver of Jie's life force from the Garden of Grotesqueries.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Our family will remember the favor, and repay it when the time is right.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I'm simply doing my duty as a Candleholder. The Supervisor lost her composure, but the candle of the Sui Regulator remains lit.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: She was trapped between the greater good and personal desire, and was eventually crushed by her own obsessions... you must not repeat her mistakes, when the time comes for you to take on great responsibility.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Now, though, it is more useful for you to go outside than to stay here and look at relics of the past.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Take this girl too, and put her somewhere safe downstairs. It is late autumn, and the floor is cold.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I trust you will handle the matter of the writing knife properly, Academician Jun.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Of course.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: One more request, if I may.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Jun turns around and picks up the geist from the ground.
She has turned back into the small, quiet doll, perhaps exhausted from the action she just performed.
You know that this geist was formed by the last speck of Jie's life force.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Wang drew you into the game and had you retrieve this object so that you could deliver it here and allow it to recuperate, in preparation for her return.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: But... consider it a burst of sentimentality from a reunion with someone long thought lost.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Please deliver it to the Tomb of Sui. The future is yet unsettled. Our family will gather outside the tomb.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I do not know if the day will come when Jie returns. But if Wang sees it as Jie's foundation, she should be there for this meeting.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Wang is in the tomb, and I must help him. There will be a battle. Carrying this object would be unsafe, and perhaps cause a delay...
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Leave it to me!
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: The Shanhaizhong snatches the token from the woman's hands before the Candleholder can react.
I will see it done, in the name of Zushi!
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: The dignified woman bows deeply to the two youngsters.
Thank you.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Lady Jun...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: You blocked the attack against me at the gate, and protected Jie's legacy.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Has chivalry become a habit of yours?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I do what I do for the sake of justice.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: What is justice?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Justice is making sure that Wei Yenwu does not die an unjust death, and giving the old case a proper trial.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Justice is bringing closure to all that has happened between mother and father, between the imperial family and the House of Ch'en.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Justice is making sure that the proxy who did so much for Great Yan did not die in vain, and giving Great Yan a chance at peace.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: What about justice for you as an Infected?
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I have seen so much suffering in my journeys... this land has been too kind to me. I wield my justice for the sake of others.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Very well.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I can't help you with the justice you seek. But perhaps her relic can.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Do you mean... the writing knife?!
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Take it.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: The justice you seek is closely linked to the incident forty years ago.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: You are the only one to whom I can entrust this knife, who can right the wrongs for your family, for your country.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: There should be closure to the matters of the Ch'en family, Lord Wei's decision, and the thousand years of records for the land on which she once lived and breathed.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: These were the hopes that she placed upon the writing knife.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: The history of Great Yan should be written by the Yanese people.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Come with me.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: They walk into the room.
Like revisiting a scene from an old play, Jun slowly walks through the room that has not changed one bit in the past century.
Everything is familiar. No one has touched it for a hundred years.
It's been a while.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: The writing knife has resurfaced, but it is no longer in your hands. If I have entrusted it to the wrong person...
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: I will shoulder the responsibility for any consequences on your behalf.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: Ch'en's palm closes around the knife. Her fingertip trembles, as if the weight of a thousand books is upon her heart.
She has given her all wholeheartedly, and sacrificed herself selflessly. Now, everything has come together as appointed.
She clutches the writing knife. In her eyes, something sinks, and something else burns bright.
—It is the weight of an era, and a reunion that the knife has long awaited.
I hope... you will not disappoint.
attributes.name: Jun

attributes.content: ...I will not disappoint.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: What's going on?!
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: These geists just appeared out of nowhere!
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: The annals of Mangshan Town record that similar scenes of rampaging geists were spotted during the turmoil caused by Sui a hundred and twenty years ago.
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: Is the bestial Sui awakening?
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: What a headache... guess it's happened after all.
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: Number Two, you fool, why are you dilly-dallying? How many times do I have to bail you out?
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: Lin Qingyan!
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: What are your instructions?
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: This is where the geists are most rampant. They must not be allowed to enter Baizao!
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: You and I will guard this pass. Do not let a single geist through!
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: Yes, master!
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: I can't hear you, girl!
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: Look lively! Treat this as your entrance exam. Pass the test, and I'll take you as my student. Then you can make your old teacher call you 'master'!
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: I wouldn't dare!
attributes.name: Lin Qingyan

attributes.content: Is there anything that I can help with?
attributes.name: Yun Qingping

attributes.content: Help? What could you do...
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: Watch my moves and record them. Then you can show them to your teacher and see who's better.
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: The fire rises—
attributes.name: 'Old Tianshi'

attributes.content: Wait... *pant* wait for me... *pant*
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: You run too fast!
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: What's the matter? You were the one who was in a hurry.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: If you're out of breath, give it to me and I'll deliver it.
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: In your dreams!
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: You blockhead! Did you think about how we're supposed to enter the Tomb of Sui when we arrive?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: We'll explain to the soldiers guarding the tomb and ask them to let us through...
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Riiiiggghhht. And how do you plan to explain where you got the thing you're holding?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Or... maybe find a way to sneak in...
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: Your brain really is made entirely of wood, isn't it?
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Do you have an idea, then?
attributes.name: Zuo Le

attributes.content: If we can't get inside the Tomb of Sui, we'll find someone who can.
attributes.name: Mo Yi

attributes.content: Do you mean...
attributes.name: Zuo Le
Zuo Le
attributes.content: Who's there—

attributes.name: Zuo Le
attributes.content: Show yourself!
Zuo Le senses a hostile presence and instinctively draws his sword.
The thing you promised me.
attributes.name: Ya

attributes.content: Hand it over.
attributes.name: Ya

attributes.content: The Forbidden City is just ahead...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: It should be heavily guarded, if the Grand Commandant really intends to sacrifice Wei Yenwu's life.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: There are guards stationed at all the important intersections in the city. It should not be hard to detect unusual movements by a large force.
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Wait, is that...
attributes.name: Ch'en

attributes.content: Ch'en stops in her tracks.
She sees a figure.
A very familiar one.
Hui-chieh.
attributes.name: Wei Yenwu

attributes.content: You should not have come.
attributes.name: Wei Yenwu
